docs.title: Group Handbook
docs.text:
This the handbook of the Neuronal Oscillations group addressing technical implementations. The aim is not to replicate existing documentation but rather to provide pathways for how-to specific to the group.

If you have ideas for improvements or new sections lets us know. The best approach for forwarding ideas is to use 'Issues' in the task bar of GitHub.

docs.title: How to set up a study
Purpose
docs.text: We will here explain the essentials for setting up a study at OHBA

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Recommendation for the analysis imaging data incluyding MEG, OPM and MRI data. We advise to use the Oxford Advanced Research Computing for data analysis. We assume try primary data to be stored on the OXford RFS. .

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: A SSO account at University of Oxford

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Recommendation for storing of OHBA imaging data incluyding MEG, OPM and MRI data. We propose of use the Oxford Research File Service (RFS) for anonymised data (e.g. raw OPM data with no identiers). OneDrive can be used for non-anonymised data.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: A SSO account at University of Oxford

docs.title: Requestions a RFS storage
docs.text: • Follow steps on https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/article/oxford-research-file-service-rfs-available-for-storing-your-live-research-data (this will take you to the Service Catalogue where RFS can be requestes)

docs.title: Mounting RFS storage
docs.text:
• ensure you are on VPN
  • Windows: In the File Explorer, click on This PC in the left hand panel. Press Map network drive, found in the ribbon. In the Folder text box, enter \connect.ox.ac.uk\. should be mapped to the R: drive if possibl
  • MAC-OS: In the Finder, select Go. Click Connect to server :

docs.title: Getting a key for link a participant data on OneDrive and RFS
Setting up a study on the Cerca OPM system
Purpose
docs.text: We will here explain the details required for setting up a study on the OPM system

docs.title: Triggers
docs.text:
We recommend using the the Pixel Mode on the VPixx hardware. This allows for driving 5 binary lines on the DataPixx parallel port which is connected to the parallel port of the Cerca system.

DataPixx DataPixx DataPixx Cerca Cerca Cerca Pin Colour Pin Bit Value Line Trigger label Code DOUT8 G0 256 D3 Trig8 DOUT10 G2 1024 D4 Trig9 DOUT12 G4 4096 D5 Trig10 DOUT14 G6 16384 D6 BNC2 DOUT16 B0 65536 D7 BNC3
